sourceMovie.title: Hitman
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Hitman for the same reason it broke through in 2007: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Xavier Gens keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gemini Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hitman clicked for you, Gemini Man (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hitman clicked for you, Gladiator (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hitman?
faq.answer: Try Gemini Man, Gladiator, Léon: The Professional—each shares DNA with Hitman while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hitman?
faq.answer: Hitman (2007) is best described as Action, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hitman part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Hitman Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
